channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge temporarily blocks restrictions
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge temporarily blocked regulations on Friday that providers said had kept them from offering the procedure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fiery Wyoming tunnel crash kills at least 2, closes I-80 in both directions
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — A fiery pileup Friday at an Interstate 80 tunnel in southwestern Wyoming killed at least two people, seriously injured at least five others and shut down the highway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why you shouldn't leave your windshield wipers up during every snowstorm
item.description: Propping windshield wipers up before a snowstorm is a common routine for many car owners, but according to AAA, it's not always the best practice.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WGN 9

item.title: Homeowner pleads guilty to shooting Black teen Ralph Yarl who rang the wrong doorbell
item.description: LIBERTY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man pleaded guilty Friday to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, in a case that shocked the country and renewed national debate about gun policies and race in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The Trump administration blocked The Associated Press from traveling with President Trump on Air Force One and also from covering an Oval Office event.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs will overturn decades of trade policy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is taking a blowtorch to the rules that have governed world trade for decades. The “reciprocal’’ tariffs that he announced Thursday are likely to create chaos for global businesses and conflict with America’s allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tigers, zebras and other stuffed animals will get new homes after health concerns kept them hidden
item.description: Crocodiles, monkeys, tigers, zebras and dozens of other taxidermy animals will move to new homes after concerns about arsenic exposure forced the closure of the South Dakota museum where they had been displayed for decades.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 people shot in Philadelphia as city celebrates Eagles victory
item.description: Two people were shot in Philadelphia Friday afternoon as the city held its Eagles Super Bowl Parade, Philadelphia Police said. The shooting happened outside of the parade footprint, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Weaver, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where US adults think the government is spending too much, according to AP-NORC polling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults believe the federal government is overspending — but polling also shows that many Americans, including Republicans, think the country is spending too little on major government programs such as Social Security.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC councilwoman offers bill to shield migrant shelter locations from public, amid illegal migration crackdown
item.description: A New York City councilwoman introduced a bill to hide the addresses of migrant shelters from the public amid a crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houston attorney Tony Buzbee expands lawsuits against Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs with 7 new cases
item.description: Houston attorney Tony Buzbee announced he has filed lawsuits for an additional seven alleged victims of Sean “Diddy” Combs.
item.dc:creator: Christian Terry, KPRC 2

item.title: Susan Smith's ex-husband says she casually apologized after killing their two sons
item.description: David Smith, ex-husband of South Carolina killer mom Susan Smith, has revealed what his ex-wife said to him after she drowned their two sons in a new 'Dateline' episode.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy will kick off his bid for Ohio governor in Cincinnati on Feb. 24
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Vivek Ramaswamy is preparing to launch his 2026 bid for Ohio governor on Feb. 24 in his native Cincinnati, with ensuing announcements held over two days in the Columbus, Toledo and Cleveland areas, The Associated Press has learned.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing man tortured and killed, 5 arrested: NY police
item.description: Five people have been arrested in connection to the death of Sam Nordquist — a Minnesota man who investigators said was tortured and killed.
item.dc:creator: George Gandy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order stripping funds from schools requiring COVID-19 vaccines
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Friday to defund schools and other education agencies that require COVID-19 vaccines for students and staff. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Trump border czar and NYC mayor interview with 'Fox & Friends' on immigration commitments
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reestablishing an immigration office at New York's notorious Rikers Island jail is just the first step in a more complex agreement President Donald Trump's administration has reached with Democratic Mayor Eric Adams, the new U.S.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Park Service removes transgender references from Stonewall National Monument website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE gets mixed reviews from conservatives who have long wanted major budget cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Conservatives who have spent decades promoting deep cuts to government are giving mixed reviews to the Department of Government Efficiency, the Elon Musk-led project to slash the federal workforce and reduce or end disfavored programs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cause of death for 'General Hospital' actress revealed
item.description: Former “General Hospital” actress Leslie Charleson’s death last month was the result of “sequelae of blunt head trauma,” according to the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner’s Office. Charleson, 79, had died on Jan. 12, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: Driver in Utah escapes car before train smashes into vehicle
item.description: Video appears to show that the SUV may have been pushed onto the tracks by another vehicle, just as the crossing gates were beginning to lower.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deadly DC plane crash investigation examining helicopter altitude
item.description: In the weeks since the crash, the NTSB has been analyzing flight data and damage to determine how this happened.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Very cold weather is coming. US about to get 10th and chilliest polar vortex this winter
item.description: The coldest burst of Arctic air this season is coming to put an icy exclamation point on America's winter of repeated polar vortex invasions, meteorologists warn. And it will stay frozen there all next week.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida newlyweds get justice after family fishing trip ended in murder
item.description: A Florida killer found guilty of killing a newlywed couple in front of their toddler was finally executed on Thursday, nearly three decades after his gruesome crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas executes man for 2004 double killing in a dispute with his strip club manager
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas man who killed his strip club manager and another man, then later prompted a massive lockdown of the state prison system when he used a cellphone smuggled onto death row to threaten a lawmaker, was executed Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA applications down in California amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The share of students with at least one undocumented parent who applied for aid dropped by nearly half.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: AI-powered fitness revolutionizing workouts with adaptive equipment
item.description: “I’m a tech nerd really at heart, and it’s cool to be on that cutting edge,” said Trevor Gere, co-owner of The Smart Fit Method in Northwest Suburban Kildeer.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, KRON4

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs off on proposed reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed a presidential memorandum proposing reciprocal tariffs that he maintains will crack down on unfair and discriminatory tariffs from both adversaries and allies.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Oklahoma governor says he'll stop plan to collect kids' immigration status
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt said he will stop a plan pushed by the state’s top education official to collect the immigration status of children when they register for public school.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices hit record high of $4.95, will likely keep climbing
item.description: Of course, that is only the nationwide average. A carton of eggs can cost $10 or more in some places. And specialized varieties, such as organic and cage-free eggs, are even more expensive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
